reason: Organizer is the durable product entity and club is one organizer subtype. The migration must replace club-specific storage, identifiers, relationships, routes, and client vocabulary without breaking released apps or public pages.

phases.description: Define the organizer taxonomy, migration aliases, audited owner-editable field, and one Host app reference flow while legacy documents default to club.
Add organizer-named callables and repositories, mirror writes into organizers, and keep bounded club-named callable wrappers for released clients.
Introduce organizerId and organizer relationship collections, dual-write all dependent documents, projections, search records, analytics dimensions, and storage paths.
Run dry-run-first idempotent tooling that copies every legacy club as organizerType club unless a canonical mapped value exists, then prove field, document, relationship, route, and media parity.
Move Flutter consumer and Hosts surfaces plus React website and admin clients to organizer-named domains, identifiers, routes, and primary reads.
Make organizers the only primary write target, freeze new clubs data, retain measured fallback reads, and verify released-client compatibility.
After remote parity and release-age evidence, remove fallback reads and legacy fields, collections, indexes, rules, storage paths, and callable wrappers through an explicitly approved cleanup.

rulesAndIndexes.firestoreRules: Organizer collection and dependent organizerId paths must be deployed before clients switch primary reads or writes.
rulesAndIndexes.firestoreIndexes: Every clubId index must gain an organizerId equivalent and pass parity checks before the old index is retired.
rulesAndIndexes.storageRules: Organizer media paths must be dual-supported and copied with checksum evidence before club media paths are frozen.

guards: A clubs-to-organizers vocabulary scanner blocks new product-facing club authority outside explicit compatibility allowlists.
Backfill apply mode is disabled until dry-run counts, conflict reports, backups, and target environment are explicit.
Legacy deletion is a separate explicitly approved operation after parity and released-client evidence.
